rules.statement: All AI invocations MUST be mediated by a PromptModel artifact. Direct calls to make_request_async() or make_request() with inline string prompts are PROHIBITED outside of the PromptModel implementation itself.
rules.rationale: Inline prompts are ungoverned, untestable, and bypass constitutional standards. PromptModels ensure every AI call carries validated context, a system prompt grounded in .intent/ policy, reference examples, and an output contract. This is the AI equivalent of 'no direct writes' — all mutations route through governed surfaces.

rules.statement: Every PromptModel MUST have a model.yaml manifest declaring: id, version, role, input.required fields, output.format, and success_criteria.
rules.rationale: An incomplete PromptModel artifact is as dangerous as an inline prompt. The manifest is the contract — without it, there is no governance.

rules.statement: Every PromptModel MUST include a system.txt file. An empty or missing system prompt defaults to 'You are a helpful assistant.' which provides zero constitutional grounding.
rules.rationale: The system prompt is where constitutional law is communicated to the AI. It is the equivalent of a role's .intent/ policy document. Omitting it removes all governance from the invocation.

rules.statement: PromptModel invocations MUST validate the AI response against the output contract defined in model.yaml before returning to the caller.
rules.rationale: An unvalidated AI response can contain violations of constitutional standards. Output validation at the PromptModel layer is the last line of defense before unvetted content enters the system.
